questions.question: Which C-string function returns the length of a string without counting the null terminator?
questions.explanation: strlen returns the number of characters in a C-string, not counting the null terminator ('\0') that marks the end of the string.

questions.question: What does the character testing function `isdigit(ch)` return when ch contains '7'?
questions.explanation: isdigit returns a non-zero (true) value if the character is a digit ('0' through '9'), and 0 (false) otherwise. '7' is a digit, so it returns true.

questions.question: Which string class method returns the number of characters currently stored in a string object?
questions.explanation: The length() method (and its synonym size()) returns the number of characters currently stored in the string object, not including any null terminator.

questions.question: What happens if you use the relational operators (==, <, >) directly on two C-string variables?
choices: It compares the strings character by character
It compares the memory addresses of the strings, not their contents
It compares the lengths of the strings
questions.explanation: Using relational operators on C-string variables compares their memory addresses (pointers), not their contents. Use strcmp to compare C-string contents.

questions.question: The C-string function ___ is used to copy one string into another.
questions.explanation: strcpy(destination, source) copies the contents of the source C-string into the destination array, including the null terminator.

questions.question: The function ___ tests whether a character is an alphabetic letter (a-z or A-Z).
questions.explanation: isalpha returns a non-zero (true) value if the character is an uppercase or lowercase letter, and 0 (false) otherwise.
